results.title: BNY Mellon AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
BNY Mellon is positioning itself as an AI-first financial services company through its comprehensive AI strategy built around its enterprise platform Eliza [1]. The bank has achieved remarkable scale with over 20,000 employees building AI agents and more than 125 AI solutions in production [2]. CEO Robin Vince emphasized that 'AI is for everyone, everywhere, everything' at BNY, with 99% of the workforce trained on AI and over 100 digital employees working alongside humans [3]. The bank has formed strategic partnerships with OpenAI and Google Cloud to enhance Eliza's capabilities, and established a $10 million AI research lab with Carnegie Mellon University [4].

AI/ML Engineer with 3+ years of experience building scalable machine learning, Generative AI, and backend engineering solutions across financial services and consumer technology domains. Currently working at BNY Mellon, developing fraud detection pipelines, semantic search systems, AI inference services, and MLOps workflows using Python, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, LangChain, MLflow, Spark, Kubernetes, and AWS SageMaker. Experienced in building Generative AI and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications using OpenAI APIs, FAISS, and NLP technologies to improve enterprise search, analytics, and automation systems. Skilled in Machine Learning, LLMs, NLP, Predictive Modeling, Recommendation Systems, FastAPI, Apache Spark, Databricks, Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud-native AI application development. results.title: Sasi Kumar
highlights:
AI/ML Engineer focused on LLMs, RAG, and secure Generative AI systems.
...
Associate Data Scientist at [BNY](https://www.linkedin.com/company/bny-mellon)
...
AI/ML engineer with over 4 years of experience designing and building intelligent, secure, and scalable AI systems. I specialize in Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), LangChain, vector embeddings, Google Gemini AI, vector databases, transformers, Mixture of Experts (MoE), GPT, prompt engineering, and fine-tuning. I also have strong expertise in machine learning and deep learning techniques, including supervised and unsupervised learning, CNNs, and RLHF. I focus on creating AI systems that are not only powerful but also safe, reliable, and efficient. I have worked on projects that combine innovation, security, and practical impact: SecureAIChatbot – Built a RAG-based chatbot using vector embeddings and semantic search. Integrated it with LLMs (Ollama) and applied Red Teaming safeguards to prevent unsafe or biased outputs. This project demonstrates my ability to build secure, real-world AI systems. PDF Chat Assistant – Developed an AI document Q&A system using LangChain, FAISS vector store, and Google Gemini AI. Implemented semantic search, multi-turn conversation memory, and scalable architecture to provide fast and accurate answers across large document sets. VoiceBot – Created an offline voice-based interview coach using Whisper and GPT4All, with text-to-speech and customizable questions. Ensured privacy and offline capability while providing intelligent, context-aware feedback. Experienced in using tools and technologies like Python, R, SQL, Tableau, Pandas, NumPy, GitHub, and Kaggle, and I continuously explore emerging AI techniques to solve real-world problems. I thrive in environments where I can turn data into actionable insights, improve AI performance, and build systems that help businesses make smarter decisions. My approach is to turn complex data into actionable insights, build scalable AI pipelines, and deliver practical solutions using advanced AI techniques. I focus on safe and responsible AI, making sure that the systems I build are robust, unbiased, and reliable. I am passionate about using RAG, LangChain, and Google Gemini AI to solve real-world problems, create innovative AI experiences, and help businesses make better decisions through intelligent, context-aware, and secure AI systems. Core Skills: Python | LLMs | RAG | LangChain | Google Gemini AI | Vector Embeddings | Vector Databases | Transformers | MoE | GPT | Prompt Engineering | Fine-Tuning | Ollama | R | SQL | Tableau | CNN | Supervised & Unsupervised Learning | RLHF | GitHub | Kaggle | Pandas | NumPy

highlights:

...
Senior Data Scientist at [BNY Investments Mellon](https://www.linkedin.com/company/mellon-investments)
...
AI/ML Engineer with 8+ years of experience designing and delivering enterprise-scale intelligent systems across banking, insurance, and technology domains. Proven expertise in building end-to-end machine learning and Generative AI solutions—from data engineering and model development to scalable deployment and MLOps automation. Specialize in Natural Language Processing, deep learning, and predictive analytics, developing advanced solutions such as RAG-based GenAI platforms, transformer-driven text analytics, recommendation engines, anomaly detection systems, and time-series forecasting models. Adept at leveraging modern AI frameworks including TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, and Hugging Face to translate complex data into actionable intelligence. Experienced in architecting scalable data and ML pipelines using Spark, Databricks, Airflow, and cloud-native services across Azure and AWS. Strong background in distributed training environments, containerized deployments (Docker/Kubernetes), and CI/CD-driven MLOps workflows using MLflow, Jenkins, and Terraform to ensure reproducibility, governance, and continuous model improvement. Passionate about building explainable, production-ready AI systems that deliver measurable business value, enhance decision-making, and drive operational efficiency. Known for collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders to transform business challenges into secure, scalable, and insight-driven solutions. ...
Innovator. Ideator. Technologist. I’m passionate about telling stories with data, uncovering insights that drive decisions, spark innovation, and create impactful products. I’ve worked across multiple industries, from optimizing energy workflows at ExxonMobil to exploring generative AI and building LLM-powered systems at Carnegie Mellon and Equiem. I am excited about learning the nuances of new businesses and applying data-driven strategies to help them grow and innovate. I thrive on curiosity, collaboration, and pushing boundaries to create customer-focused solutions. Here’s a glimpse into some of the problems I’ve loved solving: Reimagining Real Estate with AI: At Equiem, in collaboration with Carnegie Mellon University, I helped architect and launch the first
...
Concierge for 85 commercial buildings across the UK. This wasn’t just about algorithms, it was about creating a seamless experience for tenants by building systems that could adapt and respond to their needs autonomously. Protecting Coral Reefs from Space: While working with CMU’s AI Lab and VirtualGold LLC, I dived into satellite imaging to tackle one of nature’s most urgent problems—the health of coral reefs. Using predictive models, we pinpointed areas at risk, identified locations for regrowth, and built a system to recommend restoration strategies. From collaborating with tech leaders to non-profits and astronauts, this project taught me the power of data to protect what matters most. Driving Low-Carbon Growth: At ExxonMobil, I worked on data strategies to support hydrogen and carbon trading markets. I crafted pricing models that helped shape decarbonization pathways, collaborating with industries like steel and construction to make sustainability actionable. The experience showed me that even massive systems can change direction with the right data in hand. Real-Time Oil and Gas Supply Monitoring

When global operations demand instant insights, there’s no time for inefficiency

I never thought the work I did putting together BNY ’s AI lifecycle would lead off a Microsoft article, but it has! (Seriously there is so much cooler stuff here!) When I joined BNY in its early days of AI, there were quite a few reviews that covered different aspects of AI.

I architect and build enterprise-scale platforms that move risk management beyond static reporting — currently leading development of an Enterprise Risk MCP Server that provides a governed, auditable orchestration layer for AI agents across Financial and Non-Financial Risk, contributing to a unified Risk & Compliance Intelligence Platform, and developing a Swarm Intelligence Engine for cross-domain what-if scenario modeling. My work sits at the intersection of agentic AI, financial systems, and enterprise architecture — designing infrastructure that risk and compliance users can actually trust and act on. Graduate certificate candidate in Machine Learning & Data Science at Carnegie Mellon University.
...
Risk Engineering Enterprise Risk MCP Server Architected and leading development of BNY's Enterprise Risk MCP Server — a centralized governance and orchestration layer enabling secure, auditable AI agent access across Financial and Non-Financial Risk stripes. Provides a single entry point with authentication, entitlements, RBAC, and audit logging. Routes natural language queries to specialized subagents with secure access to risk data sources, semantic models, and BA tooling. Supports client platforms including the Risk & Compliance Intelligence Platform, BNY's AI platform ELIZA, and broader AI tooling. Risk & Compliance Intelligence Platform Architected and contributing full-stack development of the Risk & Compliance Intelligence Platform — consolidating Financial Risk (Credit, Market, Counterparty Credit, Treasury, Model) and Non-Financial Risk (Operational, Technology) across the enterprise. Reduces reliance on fragmented, static dashboards by centralizing risk visibility into a single dynamic platform — enabling faster decision-making for risk and compliance stakeholders. Built on a monorepo with Next.js 16, Python FastAPI microservices, PostgreSQL, and Snowflake. Contributed to CI/CD pipeline and monorepo design standards. Swarm Intelligence Engine Architecting and developing a Swarm Intelligence Engine — derived from MiroFish — spanning Credit, Market, and Model Risk for social signal analysis and emergent pattern detection for advanced what-if scenario modeling. Designed as an engine-as-a-service delivering holistic cross-domain intelligence across Financial and Non-Financial Risk models — enabling synthesis of disparate model outputs into cohesive, scenario-driven insights. Developed various agentic solutions across Risk & Compliance use cases, leveraging LLMs and multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph) to automate analysis and streamline workflows. Exploring Risk ML analysis on Nvidia B200 GPUs for anomaly detection and event-based reporting.
